name: Marisa Style (HP)
notes: 1f extra recovery on whiff; releasing the button on frames 7-17 results in uncharged version with 5f startup; great range and frame advantage for follow-up combos and pressure; puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state

name: Marisa Style (HK)
notes: Releasing the button on frames 5-13 results in uncharged version with 10f startup; puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state; Punish Counter causes free juggle state

name: Crouching Heavy Punch (Hold)
notes: Launches opponent into limited juggle state on hit, and much higher launch on Punish Counter; useful for continuing juggles; cannot hit cross-up and too slow to realistically anti-air; only first 2 active frames hit grounded opponents; active frames 3-6 only hit airborne opponents; releasing the button on frames 5-16 results in uncharged version with 4f startup; only Super cancelable on the first 2 active frames; can always combo into a fully charged SA1 for incredible 1-bar damage; hitstop is 2 frames longer on hit than on block for easier cancel hitconfirms

name: Gaia Bash (Hold)
notes: 3f extra recovery on whiff; has juggle potential and keeps the opponent in a limited juggle state (e.g. corner OTG bounce > 2[HK] > SA2); releasing the button on frames 7-15 results in uncharged version with 4f startup; topmost hurtboxes on frames 1-7 are projectile invincible (avoids Sagat's MP/HP Tiger Shot but becomes vulnerable on frame 8~)

name: Marisa Style (j.HP)
notes: Releasing the button on frames 5-19 results in uncharged version with 5f startup; causes spike knockdown; lower body projectile invuln 28-37f except around attack hitbox (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

name: Marisa Style (j.HK)
notes: Releasing the button on frames 7-19 results in uncharged version with 3f startup; causes spike knockdown; lower body projectile invuln 29-37f except around attack hitbox (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

name: Magna Bunker (Hold)
notes: 5f extra recovery on whiff; 2nd active frame is omitted on hit/block (when combined with the extra whiff recovery, the total frame count is 6f longer on whiff compared to hit/block); releasing the button on frames 5-16 results in uncharged version with 3f startup; short range but excellent combo and pressure tool; meaty connect does not give extra frame advantage; puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state

name: Malleus Breaker 1 (Hold)
notes: Slow overhead that can combo on hit or give pressure on block; releasing the button on frames 7-13 results in uncharged version with 14f startup; causes a short OTG bounce vs. airborne opponents

name: Falx Crusher 1 (Hold)
notes: Releasing the button on frames 7-14 results in uncharged version with 7f startup (frames 15-20: 4f startup); has a hitconfirmable Target Combo follow-up; forces stand on hit; puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state; more range than uncharged version with excellent follow-up combos or block pressure; special/DR cancel is delayed until after 3rd active frame

name: Caelum Arc
notes: Can hit Cross-up; can hold button 20f for improved properties; causes spike knockdown vs. airborne opponents; lower body projectile invuln 9f until land except around attack hitbox (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

name: Caelum Arc (Hold)
notes: Can hit Cross-up; releasing the button on frames 5-19 results in uncharged version with 4f startup; causes spike knockdown; lower body projectile invuln 28f until land except around attack hitbox (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

name: Gladius (Hold)
notes: Armor loses to low attacks; transitions to a cinematic follow-up on hit; vs. airborne opponents, 2nd follow-up hit whiffs (no Hard Knockdown and reduced Super Meter gain); cannot hit cross-up

name: Gladius (Hold)
notes: Armor loses to low attacks; transitions to a cinematic follow-up on hit; vs. airborne opponents, 2nd follow-up hit whiffs (no Hard Knockdown and reduced Super Meter gain); cannot hit cross-up

name: Gladius (Hold)
notes: Armor loses to low attacks; transitions to a cinematic follow-up on hit; vs. airborne opponents, 2nd follow-up hit whiffs (no Hard Knockdown and reduced Super Meter gain); cannot hit cross-up

name: Gladius (Hold)
notes: Armor loses to low attacks; transitions to a cinematic follow-up on hit; vs. airborne opponents, 2nd follow-up hit whiffs (no Hard Knockdown and reduced Super Meter gain); cannot hit cross-up

name: Scutum (Counter)
notes: Automatically triggers counterattack after 214K absorbs attack; Marisa is in a Punish Counter state for entire duration; can hold button to remain in Scutum stance after counterattack; hit advantage depends on attack that is absorbed; Marisa's short 6f hitstop causes attack to land as a +1 Counter-hit against most Medium/Heavy normals, while landing as a +3 Punish Counter against Lights

name: Scutum (Counter)
notes: Automatically triggers counterattack after 214KK absorbs attack; Marisa is in a Punish Counter state for entire duration; can hold button to remain in Scutum stance after counterattack; hit advantage depends on attack that is absorbed; Marisa's short 6f hitstop causes attack to land as a +1 Counter-hit against most Medium/Heavy normals, while landing as a +3 Punish Counter against Lights

name: Tonitrus 1
notes: 1st active frame only hits airborne opponents (functionally 8+16f startup vs. grounded); causes spike knockdown vs. airborne opponents; Marisa is in a Punish Counter state for entire duration; hitconfirm window is 1f shorter vs. grounded opponents; cannot be canceled into Tonitrus 2 on whiff; range includes 0.218 forward movement from 214K startup

name: Tonitrus 2
notes: True blockstring from overhead starter; Marisa is in a Punish Counter state for entire duration; cannot be performed when Tonitrus 1 whiffs

name: Procella
notes: Risky low ender; may catch opponent by surprise since the overhead ender is safe on block; Marisa is in a Punish Counter state for entire duration; range includes 0.218 forward movement from 214K startup

name: Enfold
notes: Marisa is in a Punish Counter state for entire duration; Range: 1.109 (final active frame: 1.164); range includes 0.218 forward movement from 214K startup

name: Javelin of Marisa
notes: Held version; only the first 3 active frames trigger cinematic hit, which deals extra damage and Drive damage; max-range non-cinematic version does not give a Hard Knockdown; if frames 1-27 absorb a strike or throw, results in Counterattack version instead; has slightly less range than the uncharged version

name: Javelin of Marisa
notes: Automatically triggers counterattack after Held SA1 armor absorbs a strike or throw on frames 1-27; can be baited with safe jump timing

name: Neutral Taunt
notes: "I may need a second helping of you!"

name: Forward Taunt
notes: "Come on, the first hit's free."; active frames refer to armored period

name: Back Taunt
notes: "This will be fun! *crack*"
